mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates on equity cash flows, increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month. This always-on trend signal reflects the persistent easing bias of the central bank during the early post-crisis period.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC over any 2-year rolling window falls below 0.03 or the proportion of positive 20-day returns during FEDFUNDS declines is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below the NAIRU threshold, labor market tightness signals above-trend growth and rising corporate profits, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over a full quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during triggered periods are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate of positive returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect broad-based consumer spending and residential investment, jointly indicating strong aggregate demand that drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher.
failure_condition: In months when both series rise month-over-month, the median forward 20-day return is not positive or is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional median (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding capacity and higher output, which in the early recovery phase bolsters investor confidence and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a horizon of one quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and subsequent 60-day SPX returns has a rank IC below 0.05 over a 5-year rolling period, or the long-short portfolio based on INDPRO momentum has negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth reflects the overall health of the economy; above-trend GDP expansion boosts corporate revenues and investor sentiment, leading to positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly GDP growth and next-quarter SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 10-year window, or the average forward return in high-GDP-growth quarters is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A large positive surprise in monthly nonfarm payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and equity prices within a week as traders react to the BLS release.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following employment reports with >150k payroll gains is not positive at a hit rate above 55% over a 3-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and consumer price index are rising simultaneously, it indicates the central bank is tightening monetary policy in response to inflationary pressure, which compresses equity valuations and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The median 20-day forward return in months with joint increases is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the long-short portfolio based on this joint signal has a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion when the unemployment rate remains very low, a further decline signals an overheated labor market that will eventually force the central bank to tighten, leading to negative forward equity returns over the next quarter as investors anticipate a slowdown.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0% and declining, the 60-day forward return is not negative on average (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year sample including multiple late-cycle episodes.
